Case title: LLM-guided reflexive content analysis and codebook redesign
Short description: Use reflexive content analysis to test whether AI support works only when human researchers redesign prompts, codebooks, and interpretive checkpoints.
Empirical unit of analysis: Text passages plus iterative prompt/codebook redesign records
Candidate roles tested: Construct boundary setter; context witness; protocol architect
Key typology question: Does the human role shift from coding passages to redesigning prompts, checkpoints, and codebooks for reflexive analysis?
Observable artifacts / traces: Prompt templates, codebook redesigns, analyst reflections, comparison outputs
Key outcomes observed: Coding fidelity, interpretive fit, transparency of analytic process
What would weaken / falsify the role cla: Simple one-shot prompting performs as well as redesigned reflexive workflows
Main confounds / risks of misreading: Method-specific guidance may overfit a narrow coding style
Why this case has high leverage: Useful for seeing whether qualitative rigor comes from the workflow redesign rather than from the model alone.
collector_notes: Generated from workbook and workbook-linked sources.
